inputs.price_source: OTC Pink aggregator quotes (Investing.com / YCharts) TPHS last trade 2026-07-17 [HUMAN REVIEW — confirm official OTC Markets closing print]
inputs.shares_source: TPHS/investor-documents/ir-tphs/3-31-26-TPHS-Financials-Press-Release-v5.14.26.pdf balance sheet, shares outstanding at March 31, 2026
inputs.cash_source: Q1 2026 press release balance sheet, cash and cash equivalents at March 31, 2026
inputs.book_value_source: TPHS/investor-documents/ir-tphs/3-31-26-TPHS-Financials-Press-Release-v5.14.26.pdf balance sheet, March 31, 2026 (stockholders' deficit / shares outstanding)
inputs.note_payable_source: Steel Promissory Note (up to $5.0 million facility), Steel Connect LLC affiliate of Steel Partners; $1.344M at 12/31/2025, $1.372M at 3/31/2026
inputs.nol_source: TPHS/investor-documents/ir-tphs/3-31-26-TPHS-Financials-Press-Release-v5.14.26.pdf, Net Operating Losses note, as of March 31, 2026
inputs.steel_ownership_source: TPHS/investor-documents/sec-edgar/8-K_20250205_rpt20250205_acc0001104659_25_009671.htm Stock Purchase Agreement, Steel IP Investments LLC purchase of 25,862,245 shares for $2,586,200
inputs.ga_run_rate_source: Q1 2026 press release, total operating expenses $126K for the quarter
inputs.other_income_source: FY2025 press release, other income line (IP licensing)
inputs.normalization_note: No operating business remains after the May 20, 2025 transfer of the 95% TPHGreenwich JV interest (New Jersey property) into TPHGreenwich Trust for the benefit of shareholders of record on that date. Current common stock carries no claim on the Trust. The company today is a Steel Partners-controlled shell holding legacy Syms/Filene's Basement intellectual property and a large federal/state net operating loss carryforward, funded by a secured promissory note from a Steel Partners affiliate.

bear.notes: [Assumption] Steel Partners keeps advancing the secured promissory note to cover general and administrative costs but never discloses a plan to use the net operating loss shell or grow IP licensing. Continued dilution from note conversion or a going-private / Form 15 deregistration event leaves minority holders with a token residual value near the cash floor.

base.notes: [Assumption] Steel Partners retains Trinity Place as a preserved-net-operating-loss shell and modestly expands intellectual property licensing income; no committed acquisition or reverse-merger transaction is disclosed as of 2026-07-17. The base payoff reflects option value on the shell itself, not a filed cash flow forecast.

bull.notes: [Assumption] Steel Partners contributes or merges an operating business into the shell to use some of the $329.9 million federal net operating loss carryforward against real taxable income, consistent with Steel Partners' history of consolidating subsidiaries under its holding structure (Handy & Harman, WebFinancial, Steel Connect). No disclosed plan as of 2026-07-17; Section 382 ownership-change rules would likely cap the annual usable amount well below the face value.

optionality_gate.notes: Stockholders' equity is already negative (book value about -$0.024 per share at March 31, 2026) and the Steel Promissory Note is secured by all company assets ahead of equity. There is no GAAP or cash floor under the common stock; downside is bounded only by zero, not by book value.

nav_overlay.source: SEC filings + IR press releases; no third-party analyses located in source_inventory scan
nav_overlay.notes: The company's only real optionality is the preserved federal/state net operating loss carryforward (~$330 million face, fully reserved with a $90.4 million valuation allowance) as a potential reverse-merger or acquisition shell for its controlling holder, Steel Partners. GAAP book value is negative and is not a floor for equity holders.

options.label: Preserved federal ($329.9M) and state ($337.4M) net operating loss carryforwards as an acquisition / reverse-merger shell
options.base_terminal_rationale: [Assumption] Modest re-rating for shell optionality; no disclosed plan, no committed transaction, and Section 382 ownership-change limits would cap annual NOL usage well below face value even if a transaction occurs.
options.evidence: TPHS/investor-documents/ir-tphs/3-31-26-TPHS-Financials-Press-Release-v5.14.26.pdf NOL note; charter 4.75% ownership-change transfer restriction preserving Section 382 rights

options.label: Filene's Basement / Stanley Blacker / Running of the Brides brand licensing
options.base_terminal_rationale: Already reflected in the small 'other income' line; immaterial ($0 to about $0.24 million per year); no separate terminal value.
options.evidence: FY2025 press release other income $239K; Q1 2026 other income $0

options.label: 95% interest in TPHGreenwich Holdings LLC (New Jersey property)
options.base_terminal_rationale: Already assigned out of the company on May 20, 2025 into TPHGreenwich Trust for the benefit of shareholders of record on that date. Beneficial interests are non-transferable, so a buyer of TPHS common stock today acquires no claim on this asset. Explicit zero for current and future common stock.

options.label: Steel Promissory Note (up to $5.0 million), secured by all company assets
options.base_terminal_rationale: A liability, not an asset. Secured claim ranks ahead of common equity in any wind-down; explicit zero equity value contribution.
options.evidence: Steel Promissory Note; $1.344M outstanding at 2025-12-31, $1.372M at 2026-03-31

option_scan.question: GAAP book misstates core assets?
option_scan.answer: Partial — the $329.9 million federal NOL is a real economic asset to a taxpaying acquirer but carries a full valuation allowance on the balance sheet
option_scan.evidence: 3/31/2026 press release NOL note; $90.4 million valuation allowance

option_scan.question: In-business loss segment?
option_scan.answer: Yes — the entire remaining company is a loss-making shell (about $0.5 to $2.0 million per year of general and administrative and non-cash charges historically) funded by the Steel Promissory Note
option_scan.evidence: Q1 2026 operating loss $126K; FY2025 operating loss $1.69 million plus a $3.09 million pension settlement charge

option_scan.question: Private or illiquid stakes below fair value?
option_scan.answer: No — the 95% Greenwich joint-venture interest (New Jersey property) was already distributed out of the company on 2025-05-20 into TPHGreenwich Trust for the benefit of then-record shareholders; it is not an asset of TPHS common stock today

option_scan.question: Transitory distribution or legal recovery?
option_scan.answer: No known litigation recovery; the pension plan termination (completed July 2025) already returned about $0.9 million of reversion cash to the company, largely offset by a $2.6 million non-cash settlement charge and $0.4 million excise tax
option_scan.evidence: FY2025 press release, Pension Settlement and Plan Asset Reversion note

option_scan.question: Embedded product option already in revenue?
option_scan.answer: Yes — Filene's Basement / Stanley Blacker brand licensing income is already the entire (small) revenue base
option_scan.evidence: FY2025 other income $239K; Q1 2026 other income $0

catalyst_paths.impact: [Assumption] Steel Partners retains the shell as a preserved-NOL vehicle and modestly expands IP licensing income; no committed transaction disclosed.

Steel Partners keeps advancing the promissory note to fund general and administrative costs with no plan to use the NOL shell; continued dilution or a going-private event leaves minority holders with a token residual value.
Steel Partners contributes or merges an operating business into the shell to use a portion of the $329.9 million federal NOL against real taxable income.

notes: No disclosed plan from Steel Partners for use of the approximately $330 million net operating loss shell; base and bull scenarios are analyst assumptions, not filed guidance — treat as [PENDING APPROVAL] for any position sizing above a token watch weight

sec-edgar folder holds filings only through the 8-K dated 2025-02-05 (Steel Partners stock purchase agreement); the FY2025 10-K and 2025-2026 10-Qs have not yet been downloaded — press releases in investor-documents/ir-tphs/ are the best available primary source for 2025-2026 financials and should be reconciled against the audited 10-K once filed
Confirm the current OTC Pink closing price and shares outstanding against the next filed 10-Q; aggregator price quotes are used here because TPHS files are not current through mid-2026 on EDGAR
TPHGreenwich Trust beneficial interests belong only to holders of record as of 2025-05-20 and are non-transferable; confirm no residual claim exists for post-transfer buyers of TPHS common stock
Monitor for a Form 15 deregistration filing and any Schedule 13D/A amendments from Steel Partners affiliates

stance_proposal.override_reason: Moat and dhando both fail (no operating business; equity has no bounded floor below the secured note). Optionality gate routes to watch even though the base dated-payoff return is modestly above zero.

valuation_method_route.label: Scarce assets, royalties, and optionality
failure_modes: gross asset value mistaken for shareholder value
operating cash flow and NAV counted twice
remote options treated as contracted cash
valuation_method_route.decision_rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

economic_ownership_map.label: Cash and working capital
economic_ownership_map.growth_driver: Near-term cash only; no Trust claim (May 2025 transfer).

required_reinvestment.treatment: explicit in driver model or included in the stated owner-cash conversion and component range

economic_ownership_map.tax_and_realization_adjustments: Run-rate G&A can consume cash quickly.
economic_ownership_map.falsifier: Cash is exhausted by G&A before any shell transaction.

economic_ownership_map.ownership_claim: Legacy Syms / Filene's IP licensing stub

economic_ownership_map.falsifier: IP income goes to zero and no replacement revenue appears.

economic_ownership_map.label: Preserved NOL shell option (Steel-controlled)
economic_ownership_map.growth_driver: Risked option on shell utility; face NOL is not equity value.

required_reinvestment.treatment: explicit in driver model or included in the stated owner-cash conversion and component range

economic_ownership_map.evidence: Federal NOL ~$329.9M and state ~$337.4M at 3/31/2026 with full valuation allowance. Steel Partners ~39.8% via Feb 2025 SPA. No disclosed acquisition plan as of 2026-07-17. Section 382 would cap annual use.

probability_and_timing.timing_basis: Five-year horizon aligned with yield-curve scenarios.
probability_and_timing.probability_basis: Base assumes modest shell optionality (~40% scenario weight in dated payoff), not face NOL value.
probability_and_timing.remaining_capital_basis: Shell is funded by Steel note advances; no large equity raise modeled in base.

economic_ownership_map.tax_and_realization_adjustments: Section 382, control, and absence of a disclosed plan widen the range.
economic_ownership_map.falsifier: Going-private / Form 15 / note foreclosure leaves minority common with near-zero residual, or Steel never uses the shell.

economic_ownership_map.label: Steel promissory note and equity deficit reserve
economic_ownership_map.ownership_claim: Steel promissory note and equity deficit reserve
economic_ownership_map.growth_driver: Senior claim haircut on common; base payoff $0.045 already nets this risk judgmentally.

required_reinvestment.treatment: explicit in driver model or included in the stated owner-cash conversion and component range

economic_ownership_map.evidence: Steel note ~$1.372M secured by all assets; stockholders' deficit ~-$1.534M (~-$0.024/sh).
economic_ownership_map.tax_and_realization_adjustments: Secured claim sits ahead of equity.
economic_ownership_map.falsifier: Note conversion or foreclosure extinguishes common ahead of any shell payoff.

universal_valuation_contract.decision_rule: No security is decision-grade until every material economic claim is valued exactly once and every material evidence blocker is resolved.

valuation_methodology.decision_rule: Use one complete component schedule for the NOL/IP shell. Dated yield-curve payoff remains the stance-gate IRR bridge.

wisdom_applied: Klarman: NOLs are options, not assets at face.
Pabrai: avoid counting a reverse-merger lottery as base value without a disclosed plan.

Component stack is designed to be consistent with the $0.045 five-year base dated payoff (~14.4%/yr stance gate).
No disclosed Steel plan; optionality can go to zero.

component_valuation_results.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

items.label: Cash and working capital
mandatory_checks: Is the valuation method appropriate for this economic claim?
Is the security's ownership claim and economic-unit denominator reconciled correctly?
Does the evidence support the low/base/high range and its timing?
Are comparable assets truly like-for-like after production, contract, location, capital, ownership, and realization adjustments?
For an option, are success probability, time to realization, and remaining owner-funded capital explicit?
Is the component additive, or already embedded in another component?
What observation would move the base estimate materially?
